get.description: Lists and filters Cloudflare Tunnels in an account.

schema.description: A user-friendly name for a tunnel.
If `true`, only include deleted tunnels. If `false`, exclude deleted tunnels. If empty, all tunnels will be included.
If provided, include only resources that were created (and not deleted) before this time. URL encoded.

The status of the tunnel. Valid values are `inactive` (tunnel has never been run), `degraded` (tunnel is active and able to serve traffic but in an unhealthy state), `healthy` (tunnel is active and able to serve traffic), or `down` (tunnel can not serve traffic as it has no connections to the Cloudflare Edge).

name.description: A user-friendly name for a tunnel.

tunnel_secret.description: Sets the password required to run a locally-managed tunnel. Must be at least 32 bytes and encoded as a base64 string.

delete.description: Deletes a private network route from an account. The CIDR in `ip_network_encoded` must be written in URL-encoded format. If no virtual_network_id is provided it will delete the route from the default vnet. If no tun_type is provided it will fetch the type from the tunnel_id or if that is missing it will assume Cloudflare Tunnel as default. If tunnel_id is provided it will delete the route from that tunnel, otherwise it will delete the route based on the vnet and tun_type.

post.description:
Create a WARP IP assignment subnet. Currently, only IPv4 subnets can be created.

**Network constraints:**
- The network must be within one of the following private IP ranges:
  - `10.0.0.0/8` (RFC 1918)
  - `172.16.0.0/12` (RFC 1918)
  - `192.168.0.0/16` (RFC 1918)
  - `100.64.0.0/10` (RFC 6598 - CGNAT)
- The subnet must have a prefix length of `/24` or larger (e.g., `/16`, `/20`, `/24` are valid; `/25`, `/28` are not)

comment.description: An optional description of the subnet.

is_default_network.description: If `true`, this is the default subnet for the account. There can only be one default subnet per account.

name.description: A user-friendly name for the subnet.

network.description: The private IPv4 or IPv6 range defining the subnet, in CIDR notation.

4XX.description: Create subnet response failure

delete.description: Delete a WARP IP assignment subnet. This operation is idempotent - deleting an already-deleted or non-existent subnet will return success with a null result.

4XX.description: Delete subnet response failure

get.description: Get a WARP IP assignment subnet.

4XX.description: Get subnet response failure

patch.description:
Updates a WARP IP assignment subnet.

**Update constraints:**
- The `network` field cannot be modified for WARP subnets. Only `name`, `comment`, and `is_default_network` can be updated.
- IPv6 subnets cannot be updated

config_src.description: Indicates if this is a locally or remotely configured tunnel. If `local`, manage the tunnel using a YAML file on the origin machine. If `cloudflare`, manage the tunnel on the Zero Trust dashboard.

conns_active_at.description: Timestamp of when the tunnel established at least one connection to Cloudflare's edge. If `null`, the tunnel is inactive.

conns_inactive_at.description: Timestamp of when the tunnel became inactive (no connections to Cloudflare's edge). If `null`, the tunnel is active.

status.description: The status of the tunnel. Valid values are `inactive` (tunnel has never been run), `degraded` (tunnel is active and able to serve traffic but in an unhealthy state), `healthy` (tunnel is active and able to serve traffic), or `down` (tunnel can not serve traffic as it has no connections to the Cloudflare Edge).

service.description: Protocol and address of destination server. Supported protocols: http://, https://, unix://, tcp://, ssh://, rdp://, unix+tls://, smb://. Alternatively can return a HTTP status code http_status:[code] e.g. 'http_status:404'.

client_id.description: UUID of the Cloudflare Tunnel connector.

client_version.description: The cloudflared version used to establish this connection.

origin_ip.description: The public IP address of the host running WARP Connector.

tunnel_originRequest.description: Configuration parameters for the public hostname specific connection settings between cloudflared and origin server.

access.description: For all L7 requests to this hostname, cloudflared will validate each request's Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion request header.

audTag.description: Access applications that are allowed to reach this hostname for this Tunnel. Audience tags can be identified in the dashboard or via the List Access policies API.

required.description: Deny traffic that has not fulfilled Access authorization.

caPool.description: Path to the certificate authority (CA) for the certificate of your origin. This option should be used only if your certificate is not signed by Cloudflare.

connectTimeout.description: Timeout for establishing a new TCP connection to your origin server. This excludes the time taken to establish TLS, which is controlled by tlsTimeout.

disableChunkedEncoding.description: Disables chunked transfer encoding. Useful if you are running a WSGI server.

http2Origin.description: Attempt to connect to origin using HTTP2. Origin must be configured as https.

httpHostHeader.description: Sets the HTTP Host header on requests sent to the local service.

keepAliveConnections.description: Maximum number of idle keepalive connections between Tunnel and your origin. This does not restrict the total number of concurrent connections.

keepAliveTimeout.description: Timeout after which an idle keepalive connection can be discarded.

matchSNItoHost.description: Auto configure the Hostname on the origin server certificate.

noHappyEyeballs.description: Disable the “happy eyeballs” algorithm for IPv4/IPv6 fallback if your local network has misconfigured one of the protocols.

noTLSVerify.description: Disables TLS verification of the certificate presented by your origin. Will allow any certificate from the origin to be accepted.

originServerName.description: Hostname that cloudflared should expect from your origin server certificate.

proxyType.description: cloudflared starts a proxy server to translate HTTP traffic into TCP when proxying, for example, SSH or RDP. This configures what type of proxy will be started. Valid options are: "" for the regular proxy and "socks" for a SOCKS5 proxy.

tcpKeepAlive.description: The timeout after which a TCP keepalive packet is sent on a connection between Tunnel and the origin server.

tlsTimeout.description: Timeout for completing a TLS handshake to your origin server, if you have chosen to connect Tunnel to an HTTPS server.
